direction_rule: from is the grammatical subject: from -relation-> to

supersedes.meaning: The source replaces the target while preserving its provenance.

supplements.meaning: The source adds non-replacing material to the target.

proves.meaning: The source proof establishes the target claim.

derived_from_campaign.meaning: The source result or artifact arose from the target campaign.

evidenced_by.meaning: The source claim is supported by the target evidence object.

same_content_as.meaning: The endpoints are content-identical views or aliases.

blocked_by.meaning: The source frontier item cannot close until the target closes.

opens_gap.meaning: The source exposes or creates the target gap.

contradicts.meaning: The source makes a claim incompatible with the target.

cites.meaning: The source cites the target without claiming logical dependence.

about.meaning: The source unit is about / concerns the target mathematical object.

co_occurs_with.meaning: The endpoints (mathematical objects) co-appear in the same artifacts; a statistical heatmap signal, not a mathematical relation.

specializes_to.meaning: The source object is a special case / restriction of the target object.

isomorphic_to.meaning: The source and target objects are isomorphic (same structure up to isomorphism).

acts_on.meaning: The source object (a group, operator, or map) acts on the target object (a space or set).

has_base.meaning: The source (total/bundle/fibered object) has the target as its base object.

quotient_of.meaning: The source object is a quotient / image of the target object.

parameterizes.meaning: The source (parameter/index/family object) parameterizes the target object.
